message.content:
# Comprehensive UI Analysis: Methamphetamine Recovery App Screen

## 1. Page Title and Navigation Structure
- **Top Navigation Bar**: Dark blue background with three pill-shaped tabs
  - "Methamphetamine" (active, blue background, white text)
  - "MDMA (Ecstasy)" (inactive, gray background, white text)
  - "Cannabis" (inactive, gray background, white text)
- **Page Title**: "Methamphetamine" in large white text
- **Subtitle**: "Also known as: Shabu, Tina, Basura, Bato, Crystal, Ice" in smaller white text
- **Bookmark/Favorite Icon**: Red circular icon with white bookmark symbol in top-right corner

## 2. Visible Text Content (Exact Transcription)

### Main Description
"Methamphetamine is a potent central nervous system stimulant that floods the brain with dopamine, norepinephrine, and serotonin at up to 10x normal levels. Chronic use causes severe dopamine receptor downregulation, neurotoxicity via oxidative stress, and gut barrier destruction through vasoconstriction of intestinal blood vessels. Recovery involves rebuilding dopaminergic function, repairing the gut-brain axis, restoring testosterone in male users, and addressing the profound anhedonia that characterizes early abstinence."

### Primary Damage Section
"Severe dopamine receptor downregulation (D2 blunting), gut dysbiosis and intestinal permeability, HPG axis suppression (low testosterone), neuroinflammation via microglial activation, sleep architecture destruction."

### Pharmacology Section
- "Mechanism: Reverses DAT and VMAT-2 transporters, causing massive dopamine efflux into synaptic cleft; also releases norepinephrine and serotonin"
- "Half-life: 10-12 hours (extended: up to 30+ hours with binge use)"
- "Onset: Smoked/injected: seconds; Oral: 15-20 minutes"

### Philippines Context Section
"Shabu remains the most widely used illicit substance in the Philippines, with meth labs historically concentrated in Metro Manila and Central Visayas. The chemsex/PNP (party and play) subculture in Metro Manila creates unique harm-reduction challenges. Local recovery is complicated by the scarcity of affordable mental health professionals, making community-based and digital support tools essential. The PDEA recovery statistics show that relapse rates exceed 60% without structured support — supplementation is one evidence-based pillar of a comprehensive recovery approach."

### Recovery Protocol Sections

#### Acute Withdrawal — The Crash (Days 0-14 days)
- **Neurochemical State**: "Dopamine levels near zero. CRF (corticotropin-releasing factor) hyperactive. Sleep is severely disrupted — minimal REM. Microglia activated (neuroinflammation). Gut permeability is high."
- **Priority Supplements**:
  - "Magnesium Glycinate: 300-400mg elemental magnesium"
  - "L-Glutamine: 5-10g"
  - "Probiotic Complex: 1-2 capsules (10-50 billion CFU)"
  - "Prebiotic Inulin: 5-10g"
  - "Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine): 25-50mg"
  - "N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC): 600-1200mg"
  - "Buko Juice (Coconut Water): 1-3 glasses as needed"
- **Dietary Focus**:
  - "High protein (chicken, fish, eggs) for amino acid replenishment"
  - "Bananas (potassium, tryptophan, B6)"
  - "Malunggay (nutrient density, folate, iron)"
  - "Frequent small meals — appetite may be low"
  - "Avoid sugar spikes — they worsen dopamine crashes"
- **Milestones**:
  - "first_24h_clean"
  - "first_week_complete"
  - "sleeping_without_substance"

#### Early Recovery — Rebuilding the Foundation (Days 14-60 days)
- **Neurochemical State**: "Dopamine receptors beginning to upregulate slowly. HPA axis still dysregulated. Cortisol elevated. Anhedonia is primary symptom. Gut lining starting to repair with proper support."
- **Priority Supplements**:
  - "Magnesium Glycinate: 300-400mg elemental magnesium"
  - "L-Tyrosine: 500-1500mg"
  - "Omega-3 Fish Oil: 2000-4000mg combined EPA/DHA"
  - "Probiotic Complex: 1-2 capsules (10-50 billion CFU)"
  - "L-Glutamine: 5-10g"
  - "Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine): 25-50mg"
  - "N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC): 600-1200mg"
  - "Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma Longifolia): 200-400mg"
- **Dietary Focus**:
  - "Tyrosine-rich foods (eggs, chicken, dairy, almonds)"
  - "Omega-3 sources (bangus, galunggong, sardines, 3x/week)"
  - "Complex carbohydrates (brown rice, camote) for sustained energy"
  - "Atchara and fermented foods for gut health"
  - "Hydration: 2-3L water + buko juice daily"
- **Milestones**:
  - "two_weeks_clear"
  - "first_cravings_resisted"
  - "gut_healing_underway"
  - "sleep_improving"

#### Rebuilding Phase — Neuroplasticity Window (Months 2-6 days)
- **Neurochemical State**: "Dopamine receptors significantly upregulating. BDNF production increasing. Neuroplasticity is high — the brain is actively rewiring. HPA axis starting to normalize. Mood more stable but still fragile."
- **Priority Supplements**:
  - "L-Tyrosine: 500-1500mg"
  - "Omega-3 Fish Oil: 2000-4000mg combined EPA/DHA"
  - "Creatine Monohydrate: 3-5g"
  - "Mucuna Pruriens: 250-500mg"
  - "Ashwagandha (Withania Somnifera): 300-600mg"
  - "Turmeric / Curcumin: 500-1000mg curcumin"
  - "Tongkat Ali (Eurycoma Longifolia): 200-400mg"
  - "Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine): 25-50mg"
- **Dietary Focus**:
  - "Brain-supportive foods: fatty fish, walnuts, blueberries (if available)"
  - "High-quality protein at every meal"
  - "Luyang dilaw (turmeric) in cooking daily"
  - "Exercise to boost BDNF — even walking helps"
  - "Reduce caffeine to 1 cup/day to protect sleep quality"
- **Milestones**:
  - "two_months_milestone"
  - "anhedonia_improving"
  - "exercise_routine_established"
  - "first_moment_of_joy"

#### Maintenance — Long-Term Recovery (Month 6+ days)
- **Neurochemical State**: "Dopamine function approaching 70-80% of baseline. Neuroplasticity stabilizing. Gut-brain axis largely restored. Stress response normalized. Occasional cravings but manageable."
- **Priority Supplements**:
  - "Omega-3 Fish Oil: 2000-4000mg combined EPA/DHA"
  - "Magnesium Glycinate: 300-400mg elemental magnesium"
  - "Probiotic Complex: 1-2 capsules (10-50 billion CFU)"
  - "Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine): 25-50mg"
  - "Turmeric / Curcumin: 500-1000mg curcumin"
  - "Creatine Monohydrate: 3-5g"
- **Dietary Focus**:
  - "Mediterranean-style diet adapted to PH: fish, vegetables, olive oil"
  - "Continue fermented foods (atchara, kimchi) for gut maintenance"
  - "Antioxidant-rich fruits: papaya, mango, guava"
  - "Maintain regular sleep schedule"
  - "Social meals — connection supports recovery"
- **Milestones**:
  - "six_months_clear"
  - "dopamine_rebuilding"
  - "career_or_study_focus"
  - "giving_back"

## 3. Categories, Subcategories, and Menu Items
- **Main Categories**:
  - Primary Damage
  - Pharmacology
  - Philippines Context
  - Recovery Protocol (with 4 subcategories)
- **Recovery Protocol Subcategories**:
  - Acute Withdrawal — The Crash
  - Early Recovery — Rebuilding the Foundation
  - Rebuilding Phase — Neuroplasticity Window
  - Maintenance — Long-Term Recovery

## 4. Charts, Graphs, Data Visualizations, or Progress Indicators
- No visible charts or graphs
- Progress indicators: Milestone checkboxes (✓) next to each milestone item
- Time duration indicators for each recovery phase (e.g., "Days 0-14 days", "Days 14-60 days")

## 5. Interactive Elements
- **Tabs**: Three pill-shaped tabs at top for navigation between substances
- **Expand/Collapse Arrows**: Small upward-pointing arrows next to each recovery phase title
- **Bookmark Icon**: Red circular icon with white bookmark in top-right corner
- **Milestone Checkboxes**: Checkmarks (✓) indicating completed milestones
- **Supplement Cards**: Individual supplement information cards with dosages
- **Dietary Focus Items**: Green-colored text items with specific dietary recommendations
- **Bottom Navigation**: Partially visible navigation icons at bottom (home, search, favorites, profile)

## 6. Color Scheme and Visual Design Elements
- **Background**: Dark navy blue (#1a2332 or similar)
- **Text Colors**:
  - White for main text
  - Light gray for secondary text
  - Red for warning/primary damage section
  - Blue for pharmacology section
  - Orange/brown for Philippines context
  - Green for dietary focus items
- **Section Headers**:
  - Primary Damage: Dark red background with white text
  - Pharmacology: Blue background with white text
  - Philippines Context: Brown/orange background with white text
  - Recovery Phases: Colored pills (red, orange, blue, green) with white text
- **Cards**: Semi-transparent dark blue cards with rounded corners
- **Borders**: Subtle borders around cards and sections
- **Fonts**: Sans-serif font, varying sizes for hierarchy

## 7. Data Organization and Presentation
- **Section Organization**: Information grouped into logical sections with colored headers
- **Recovery Phases**: Presented as expandable/collapsible sections with colored pills
- **Supplements**: Displayed in a grid layout (2 columns) within each recovery phase
- **Dietary Focus**: Listed as bullet points with green text
- **Milestones**: Listed with checkmarks indicating completion status
- **Neurochemical State**: Presented as descriptive text in each recovery phase

## 8. Screen Layout Structure
- **Header**: Top navigation tabs and page title
- **Body**: Main content area with multiple sections
  - Main description
  - Primary Damage section
  - Pharmacology section
  - Philippines Context section
  - Recovery Protocol section (with 4 expandable phases)
- **Footer**: Partially visible bottom navigation bar

## 9. Numbers, Statistics, and Metrics
- "10x normal levels" (dopamine, norepinephrine, serotonin)
- "60%" (relapse rates without structured support)
- "70-80%" (dopamine function baseline in maintenance phase)
- Supplement dosages (e.g., "300-400mg", "5-10g", "25-50mg")
- Time durations (e.g., "10-12 hours", "Days 0-14 days", "Months 2-6 days")

## 10. Icons and Their Meanings
- **Bookmark Icon**: Red circular icon with white bookmark (save/favorite function)
- **Expand/Collapse Arrows**: Small upward-pointing arrows (expand/collapse sections)
- **Checkmarks (✓)**: Indicate completed milestones
- **Warning Icon**: Red triangle with exclamation mark (Primary Damage section)
- **Pill/Container Icon**: Blue icon (Pharmacology section)
- **Flag Icon**: Small flag (Philippines Context section)
- **Lightbulb Icon**: Blue icon (Neurochemical State subsections)
- **Pill Bottle Icon**: Blue icon (Priority Supplements subsections)
- **Leaf Icon**: Blue icon (Dietary Focus subsections)
- **Target Icon**: Blue icon (Milestones subsections)

## Additional UI Details
- **Status Bar**: Top of screen shows time (10:30), battery (91%), signal strength, and other standard mobile indicators
- **Spacing**: Generous padding and margin between sections for readability
- **Hierarchy**: Clear visual hierarchy with larger text for titles, colored sections for categorization, and consistent spacing
- **Responsive Design**: Content appears to be organized for mobile viewing with appropriate touch targets and readability
